mechanism_narrative: A high absolute level of the federal funds rate directly increases the cost of capital for corporations and the opportunity cost of holding equities, which mechanically lowers equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, or the Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and collapsing consumer demand, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window.
failure_condition: After UNRATE triggers, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio during trigger windows exceeds the unconditional Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and retail sales reflects a broad-based collapse in both manufacturing and consumer demand, which jointly compresses corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the interaction dominates because either variable alone may reflect sector-specific noise.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and RSAFS deciles produces a Rank IC above zero over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns following joint declines are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by elevated policy rates, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal persistent inflation that prevents monetary easing, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days because higher inflation without offsetting rate cuts erodes real earnings.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS above 4%, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC of CPIAUCSL conditional on FEDFUNDS > 4% is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and falling corporate operating leverage, which reduces earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative momentum in INDPRO is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of rolling 12-month periods, or the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling retail sales identifies a simultaneous deterioration in labor income and consumer spending that amplifies recession risk beyond either indicator alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through aggregate demand collapse.
failure_condition: The bivariate UNRATE-RSAFS signal produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of joint deterioration episodes, or the interaction term rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below the 1500 thousand unit threshold signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants downgrade cyclical earnings.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1500, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the hit rate of negative returns drops below 45% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A high and sticky level of the federal funds rate combined with elevated consumer prices restricts corporate pricing power and compresses profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: The joint signal from FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL levels produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of high-rate high-inflation observations, or the rank IC of either proxy is positive over any 3-year window.
